content: Canonical schema: tenants, sessions, skills, rate-limits, cost-tracking, audit. All use TENANT#{id} as PK. See ClawCore-Final-Architecture-Plan.md

title: Project codename: AWS Chimera
rationale: Multi-formed Greek creature reflects multi-tenant, multi-agent, multi-platform, self-evolving nature. AWS internal codename style (Bedrock, Mantle, Kuiper). CLI: chimera deploy/agent/skill.

content: Multi-tenant isolation enforcement: Never rely on FM reasoning for authorization decisions (prompt injection vulnerability). Use deterministic enforcement: IAM policies, Cedar policies, DynamoDB condition expressions, S3 bucket policies. AgentCore Memory namespace must be validated at runtime with assertions.

name: multi-table-dynamodb-design
description: DynamoDB schema design for multi-tenant SaaS: Use 6-table design over single-table when different concerns need different TTL policies, encryption requirements, and access patterns. Enhanced tenant config uses multi-item pattern (TENANT#{id} with SK=PROFILE|CONFIG#features|CONFIG#models) for atomic config updates and flexible schema evolution.
GSI cross-tenant data leakage: Global secondary indexes on shared DynamoDB tables can leak data across tenants if not filtered at application layer.
resolution: Add FilterExpression='tenantId = :tid' to all GSI queries. Document in validation report with code examples. Add integration test to verify GSI isolation.

content: AgentCore Runtime sessions are ephemeral with no persistent filesystem. Use S3 for agent workspace persistence, not EFS. Files written during a session are lost on termination unless explicitly saved to S3.

AgentCore Memory namespace template: 'tenant-{tenant_id}-user-{user_id}' for cross-tenant isolation. Memory content is stored in AgentCore managed service, not DynamoDB. DynamoDB stores memory_id and namespace_template only.

name: multi-tenant-endpoint-isolation
description: Pool/Hybrid tiers share a single AgentCore Runtime endpoint ARN. Premium/Silo tier gets dedicated endpoint ARN per tenant. Map tenant_id → endpoint_arn in DynamoDB tenants table for routing decisions.

title: AgentCore Memory strategy tier mapping
rationale: Cost optimization: Basic tier uses only SUMMARY strategy (~/tenant/mo), Advanced adds USER_PREFERENCE (~/tenant/mo), Premium uses full suite including SEMANTIC + EPISODIC (~/tenant/mo). Balances capability vs cost per tier.
